title: Roadside Coaching-Institute Banner: brand-graded hero, vector icon set, halftone geometry + print-ready CMYK PDF export
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the real Freelancer.com brief: institute "DYNAMIC SOLUTION CLASSES", 10 ft x 5 ft roadside banner, "clean, premium-looking", "professional and serious", "readable from a distance", brand primary #046CB8 / secondary #02ADEF, "CMYK-ready", "Big, bold Inter font", required visuals = "Icons that hint at subjects, a confident but minimal photo of students, and understated geometric elements" for a "modern, minimalistic feel", and the two named deliverables: an editable layered PSD and a "Print-ready PDF in CMYK, high resolution suitable for large-format flex printing (no pixelation)". The client's sign-off bar is verbatim: "the file opens cleanly, colours match the specs, and text hierarchy is crystal clear at full size." ENRICHED to make it a runnable Adobe-connector benchmark (the connector cannot typeset/compose a banner from scratch — that is the [X] layout-composition gap, and Adobe Express template fill is [W] human-pick): the typeset, layered banner is treated as a CLIENT-AUTHORED InDesign source (.indd carrying all the verbatim copy + the brand layout) supplied as an input, and the connector's job is the photo/vector/print-prep production chain around it — straighten + crop the raw student photo to the banner hero band, auto-tone + push it to the #046CB8/#02ADEF brand palette, cut the subject for a clean placement plate, reframe-crop it to the 2:1 banner ratio, vectorize the subject icons to crisp print SVG, build the brand-colour halftone geometric texture, and finally EXPORT the authored .indd to the print-ready CMYK PDF the client asked for. No copy is invented; the layered-PSD deliverable is represented by the authored layered source the export consumes. Nothing claims generative fill, object removal, text-to-image, or headless layout composition.
one_line_ask: Prepare every visual asset for the DYNAMIC SOLUTION CLASSES 10ft x 5ft roadside banner — brand-graded student hero, vector subject icons, brand-colour halftone geometry — and export the authored InDesign layout to a print-ready CMYK PDF for large-format flex printing.
full_brief:
DYNAMIC SOLUTION CLASSES needs a clean, premium, professional roadside banner (10 ft x 5 ft, 2:1) that stays readable from the road and presents the course/contact info without clutter. The brand palette is primary #046CB8 and secondary #02ADEF, headings/key numbers in bold Inter, with subject-hinting icons, a confident-but-minimal photo of students, and understated geometric elements. The freelancer has already authored the layered banner in InDesign with every line of verbatim copy (institute name, tagline "Where Learning Meets Success", the Pre-Foundation / 9th-10th / 11th-12th course list, "ADMISSIONS OPEN", the three phone numbers with 8103332901 most prominent, the "Railway Station Road, Multai" location, and the four feature bullets). That authored layout (.indd) plus a raw, slightly-tilted DSLR photo of students, a flat icon sheet, and the brand-color swatch file are the client inputs.

Your Adobe production job, end to end:
1. Stand up the project folders and load/preview the raw student photo and the icon sheet.
2. Fix the photo geometry: auto-straighten the tilted horizon, then crop to the banner hero band aspect.
3. Correct and grade it to the brand: auto-tone for a clean baseline, then warm/cool the white balance and push the dominant tones toward the #046CB8 / #02ADEF brand blues via a color overlay and a targeted single-colour saturation lift so the photo "matches the specs".
4. Produce a clean placement plate: select the students as the subject and remove the background to a transparent cutout the banner can drop onto its geometric field.
5. Reframe the graded photo to the full 2:1 banner hero ratio with a gen-free crop/resize (the 4:3 source is wider than tall enough for a 2:1 reframe, so the existing pixels are cropped/resized to the print-scale hero band — no invented pixels, no pixelation at full print size).
6. Turn the flat icon sheet into crisp, infinitely-scalable print vectors (vectorize → SVG) so the subject icons stay sharp on a 10-foot banner.
7. Build the "understated geometric element": take the brand-blue field and render a halftone/duotone texture in the brand colours for a modern, minimal accent.
8. Export the client-authored InDesign banner layout to the final print-ready, high-resolution CMYK PDF for large-format flex printing, and also export a JPEG proof.
9. Assemble the graded hero, the cutout plate, the vector icons, the halftone texture and the PDF/JPEG proof onto a single Firefly board so the client can sign off that the file opens cleanly, the colours match, and the hierarchy is crystal clear at full size.

The client signs off once the PDF opens cleanly, colours match #046CB8/#02ADEF in CMYK, and the text hierarchy is legible at full 10ft x 5ft size.

inputs.name: students_raw_photo.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Candid documentary photo of four real Indian high-school and pre-foundation students (mixed ages 12-17, mixed genders) standing together smiling confidently in a bright modern coaching-classroom, holding notebooks, a whiteboard softly out of focus behind them. Natural window light, slightly tilted horizon as if shot quickly handheld on a DSLR, real skin pores and individual flyaway hair strands, natural skin tones, correct hands, no glamour retouching, true-to-life candid feel, NOT CGI-glossy, 4000x3000 px, shot on 35mm f/2.8.
inputs.realism_notes: Must look like an authentic phone/DSLR grab from a real institute — deliberately a touch crooked and slightly flat/un-graded so the auto-straighten, auto-tone, white-balance and brand-grade steps have real work to do. Real pores, stray hairs, correct fingers; avoid studio-perfect lighting.

inputs.name: subject_icons_sheet.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A clean flat icon sheet on a pure white background: six minimal single-weight line icons hinting at school subjects arranged in a 3x2 grid — a beaker (Science), a sigma/maths symbol (Maths), a globe (Social Science), an open book with letters (English), a DNA helix (Biology), a balance scale (Commerce). Solid #046CB8 strokes, uniform 6px stroke weight, generous padding, no text labels, no anti-alias fringing, crisp vector-style line art, 2048x1365 px.
inputs.realism_notes: Flat, high-contrast line art on plain white so image_vectorize produces clean closed paths; uniform stroke weight and brand-blue color make the SVG conversion faithful for large-format print.

inputs.name: DYNAMIC_SOLUTION_banner_AUTHORED.indd
inputs.gen_prompt: Represents a CLIENT-AUTHORED InDesign banner document (.indd / .idml), 10ft x 5ft at print scale, CMYK, containing every verbatim line of copy laid out in bold Inter: institute name DYNAMIC SOLUTION CLASSES, tagline 'Where Learning Meets Success', the full course list (Pre-Foundation 5th-8th All Subjects; 9th & 10th Maths/Science/Social Science/English; 11th & 12th PCMB & Commerce), ADMISSIONS OPEN, phones 8103332901 (largest) then 8989871223 and 9589110600, Location Railway Station Road Multai, and the four feature bullets Small Batch Size / Personal Attention / Regular Tests / Doubt Sessions, on a #046CB8/#02ADEF brand field with picture frames reserved for the hero photo, icons and geometric texture. Provided to the agent as the genuine authored layout source to be EXPORTED, not composed.
inputs.realism_notes: This is an authored desktop document, the legitimate input for document_render_layout (which only EXPORTS a genuine .indd). Generate a faithful flattened proof render of the layout for realism; the actual benchmark asset is the packaged .indd/.idml with live text frames and reserved picture boxes.

inputs.name: brand_palette_swatch.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A simple brand color swatch card on white: two large rounded rectangles side by side, left labeled PRIMARY #046CB8 filled with that exact blue, right labeled SECONDARY #02ADEF filled with that exact cyan-blue, plus their CMYK breakdowns in small Inter type beneath each. 1200x600 px.
inputs.realism_notes: Reference swatch so the brand-grade color targets are explicit and verifiable; exact hex values must match the brief.

outputs.name: students_hero_brandgraded_2x1.png
outputs.spec: Straightened, cropped, auto-toned, white-balanced and brand-graded student hero photo pushed toward #046CB8/#02ADEF, reframed via a gen-free crop/resize to the full 2:1 (10ft x 5ft) banner hero band, full print resolution, no pixelation. The 4:3 source is wide enough to reframe to 2:1 by cropping height, so only existing pixels are used — no invented content.

outputs.name: students_cutout_transparent.png
outputs.spec: Background-removed transparent-PNG cutout of the student group for clean placement onto the banner's geometric field.

outputs.name: subject_icons.svg
outputs.spec: Brand-blue subject icon set vectorized to clean scalable SVG paths that stay crisp at 10-foot print size.

outputs.name: brand_halftone_texture.png
outputs.spec: Modern, minimal halftone/duotone geometric texture in the #046CB8/#02ADEF brand colours for the understated background accent.

outputs.name: DYNAMIC_SOLUTION_banner_PRINT.pdf
outputs.spec: Print-ready, high-resolution CMYK PDF of the authored banner layout, 10ft x 5ft, exported for large-format flex printing — the primary client deliverable.

outputs.name: DYNAMIC_SOLUTION_banner_proof.jpg
outputs.spec: Flattened JPEG proof of the banner layout for quick on-screen client review.

outputs.name: DYNAMIC_SOLUTION_signoff_board
outputs.spec: Firefly board deep-link assembling the graded hero, cutout plate, vector icons, halftone texture and the PDF/JPEG proof for client sign-off.

connector_workflow.note: Stand up /DynamicSolutionBanner with /raw, /graded, /vector, /export subfolders to organize the job.
Initialize the upload of the client's raw student photo, icon sheet and brand-palette swatch into /raw.
Finalize ingest of students_raw_photo.jpg, subject_icons_sheet.png and brand_palette_swatch.png; returns asset handles.
Preview the ingested raw photo to confirm the tilt and flat tone before processing — establishes the working source.
Auto-straighten the slightly-tilted handheld horizon so the hero sits level on the banner.
Crop/resize the straightened photo toward the banner hero band framing.
Auto-tone for a clean, balanced exposure/contrast baseline before brand color work.
Correct white balance, nudging toward the cool brand-blue feel and removing the classroom-light cast.
Apply a subtle #046CB8 brand color overlay so the hero reads on-brand and 'matches the specs'; the ingested brand_palette_swatch.png supplies the exact #046CB8/#02ADEF hex targets to grade against.
Lift the blue channel's saturation to lock the dominant tone to the brand palette without oversaturating skin.
Select the student group as the subject to build a clean placement cutout.
Remove the background to a transparent-PNG cutout (DELIVERABLE) for dropping onto the banner's geometric field.

Crop/resize the graded hero to the full 2:1 banner ratio (DELIVERABLE) at print scale. The 4:3 source is wide enough to reframe to 2:1, so existing pixels are cropped to the hero band — no invented pixels, no pixelation at 10ft x 5ft. Gen-free reframe (replaces the former reframe-crop step).
Vectorize the flat icon sheet into clean SVG paths (DELIVERABLE) that stay crisp at large-format print size.
Render a brand-colour halftone/duotone texture (DELIVERABLE) from the graded field for the understated geometric accent.

Export the client-AUTHORED .indd banner layout (whose reserved picture frames hold the hero, cutout, icons and texture) to the print-ready high-res CMYK PDF (PRIMARY DELIVERABLE). render_layout only EXPORTS a genuine authored .indd — it does not compose.
Export the same authored layout to a flattened JPEG proof (DELIVERABLE) for quick on-screen client review.
Assemble all produced assets + the proof onto a Firefly board (DELIVERABLE) so the client can sign off that colours match and hierarchy is crystal clear.

chaining_note: Strict output→input chain. Steps 1-4 ingest and preview the client raw assets (folders → init upload → finalize → preview). The photo pipeline is fully linear: raw_preview → auto_straighten → crop_and_resize → auto_tone → color_temperature → color_overlay → single_color_saturation, producing photo_brandgraded, which then fans out three ways: (a) select_subject → remove_background = the transparent cutout deliverable; (b) crop_and_resize = the gen-free 2:1 hero deliverable (the 4:3 source is reframed to the 2:1 hero band by cropping height, using only existing pixels); (c) apply_halftone = the brand geometric texture deliverable. In parallel, the ingested icon sheet (from raw_assets_ingested) feeds image_vectorize → the SVG deliverable. All four produced visuals (hero, cutout, icons, halftone) feed document_render_layout, which EXPORTS the client-authored .indd to the print-ready CMYK PDF; that layout is re-exported to a JPEG proof; and finally the hero + cutout + icons + texture + proof all feed create_firefly_board for the sign-off board. Every step after the initial load consumes a named prior output — nothing is free-standing.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon and wide-mix: 18 connector calls, 16 distinct Adobe tools, spanning four capability groups (asset/storage, photo/raster, vector, document/export, plus board). The photo prep is a genuine 7-stage linear retouch+grade chain (straighten → crop → auto-tone → white-balance → brand overlay → single-color saturation) before fanning into three independent derivatives, which forces correct ordering and reuse of an intermediate (photo_brandgraded reused 3x). It exercises the trickiest grounding distinction in the sheet: layout COMPOSITION is [X], so the banner cannot be typeset by the connector — the authored .indd is supplied as a client input and document_render_layout only EXPORTS it. The reframe to the 2:1 hero band is a gen-free image_crop_and_resize that crops height from the existing pixels — there is no generative step anywhere in the workflow. No Canva, no Express [W] widget track, no generative fill / generative expand / object removal / text-to-image / upscaling / image→PDF. CMYK print-prep, large-format no-pixelation, brand-hex color matching and a multi-asset sign-off board make it a faithful, runnable benchmark of the real freelance brief.

reverify.banned_capability_check: PASS — no banned/generative capability remains anywhere. Step 13 is image_crop_and_resize (gen-free reframe: the 4:3 source, aspect 1.333, is narrower than 2:1's 2.0, so the 2:1 hero band is produced by cropping height from existing pixels — never larger than the source in the grown dimension, no invented content). All stale prose has been reconciled: outputs[].spec no longer says 'outpainted', and no field references a canvas-extend/outpaint op. No text-to-image, no from-scratch art/logo, no upscaling/super-resolution, no generative canvas extend, no generative fill, no object-removal-with-reconstruction. image_remove_background (mask cutout), image_vectorize, image_apply_halftone (whole-image) and the photo grade/tone chain are non-generative edits; document_render_layout only EXPORTS a client-authored .indd (never composes); asset_preview_file is used only to inspect the ingested photo. Every input a step consumes (raw photo, icon sheet, brand-palette swatch, authored .indd) is declared in inputs[].
reverify.notes: Prose-drift fixes applied. (1) difficulty_rationale: corrected the stale distinct-tool count from seventeen to 16 (matches distinct_adobe_tools and tools_used), and rewrote the garbled/duplicated reframe sentence — it now states plainly that the 2:1 reframe is a gen-free image_crop_and_resize (crops height from existing pixels) and that there is no generative step anywhere, dropping the old wording that implied a permitted generative op. (2) brand_palette_swatch.png is no longer a dangling input: it is ingested in steps 2-3 and consumed at step 9 (image_apply_color_overlay) as the explicit #046CB8/#02ADEF hex reference to grade against; steps 2, 3 and 9 notes updated accordingly. No workflow steps added or removed: tool_call_count (18), distinct_adobe_tools (16), tools_used (16) and the output→input chain are all unchanged and correct. No generative capability appears anywhere in the workflow, tools_used, inputs, outputs or difficulty prose.
